fitScoreNote: The JD asks candidates to highlight which industry they are most interested in innovating. For my application exercise, I didn't write about a hobby - I used Perplexity itself to argue that Perplexity should build the MCP-native healthcare agent data layer, backed by investor and partner memos I wrote over two years of independent research. I've spent three years building production agentic AI systems with real eval infrastructure, designing feedback loops that fed directly into product iteration, and making nondeterministic AI legible to both engineers and executives. My formal PM title is shorter than the JD targets, but the substance - owning product direction, sequencing against user signal, making tradeoffs under uncertainty - drove 63% of net new ARR as Lead TPM. The thesis I submitted is not something I read about. It is something I have been building toward.

coreJobFunction.rationale: Dan has strong overlap on several JD requirements: deep data/analytics background (FanDuel, Action Network, Google), hands-on building of data-driven feedback loops and dashboards, experience with nondeterministic AI systems (hybrid-RAG, eval engineering, determinism-as-design-principle at Thoughtful), and his core superpower of bridging technical concepts with non-technical stakeholders is extensively documented. However, this role is specifically a Product Manager role requiring 4+ years of formal PM experience with ownership over product vision, roadmap, and PLG/retention strategy — Dan's PM tenure was brief (~3 months as formal TPM before becoming a delivery/sales engineering leader), and his day-to-day motion has been pre-sales, solutions engineering, customer delivery, and analytics rather than owning a product from vision through growth metrics. The knowledge work and productivity software domain expertise Perplexity emphasizes is also largely absent from his background, which is rooted in healthcare RCM and sports media.

seniority.rationale: Dan has ~8 years of post-college experience with strong end-to-end ownership in analytics, TPM, and sales engineering roles, meeting the 4+ year PM threshold and demonstrating data-driven product thinking, cross-functional leadership, and conviction-based decision-making. His Lead TPM role at Thoughtful shows direct product ownership (63% of net new ARR, leading FDEs, building eval frameworks for nondeterministic AI), and his healthcare agent data layer project shows genuine agentic AI product vision. However, his background is TPM and analytics rather than pure consumer/PLG product management, and he lacks direct experience with product-led growth loops, retention optimization, and the search/knowledge work domain that Perplexity's role centers on.

technicalSkills.rationale: Dan has strong adjacent credentials — deep enterprise domain expertise (healthcare AI, 61 enterprise deals), genuine hands-on AI/ML experience (hybrid-RAG systems, LLM agents, embedding models, multi-agent pipelines), and data analytics depth (SQL, cohort modeling, metrics frameworks at FanDuel/Google/Action Network). However, his title history is Solutions Architect, Customer Engineer, Lead TPM, and Director of Sales Engineering — not Product Manager. He has zero documented PM tenure against the 4+ year requirement, and no evidence of owning product vision, running user research, managing product roadmaps, or building product-led growth flywheels as a PM. The TPM role (lasting ~3 months before promotion) involved delivery and implementation judgment, not product strategy ownership. The technical AI understanding is genuinely strong, but the core PM experience gap is a hard structural miss for this role.

industryVertical.rationale: Perplexity is a horizontal AI productivity platform targeting knowledge workers broadly — not a specific industry vertical — so this scores on ICP match. Dan's career has been almost entirely healthcare RCM automation and enterprise B2B sales engineering, with earlier roles in consumer sports analytics (FanDuel) and media affiliate analytics (Action Network). None of these map closely to Perplexity's core ICP of individual knowledge workers and prosumer/consumer AI users. Dan has genuine PM-adjacent experience (Lead TPM, product roadmap input, agentic AI system building) and has used and built agentic AI tools, but his buyer personas and product contexts are healthcare enterprise and vertical SaaS — not the horizontal productivity and search consumers Perplexity serves.

logistics.rationale: Dan is a current SF resident (Richmond neighborhood) with deep Bay Area roots since 2020, making the SF/Palo Alto office location a zero-friction match. The JD states no explicit travel requirement, no security clearance, no language requirement, and no compensation band that could create misalignment; Dan's baseline openness to hybrid/in-office arrangements aligns directly with Perplexity's known in-person culture. The only minor consideration is a potential Palo Alto commute, but that is a routine Bay Area scenario, not a meaningful blocker.

summary: Built a hybrid-RAG dental eligibility system from prototype to 100% accuracy on a labeled holdout dataset in production, including embedding pipelines, vector retrieval, in-context learning, and regression tests I wrote myself.

Drove 63% of net new ARR as Lead TPM at Thoughtful AI by owning product direction, scope tradeoffs, and cross-functional delivery across 61 enterprise deals in a high-stakes healthcare AI environment.

Designed a pre/post tool-use hook feedback system at Smarter Technologies that logged every agent skill invocation, surfaced user ratings on 15-20% of uses, and fed directly into iteration cycles - a production data flywheel built from scratch.

Created the Value Clicks north star metric at Action Network from scratch, connecting editorial behavior to affiliate LTV, which directly reshaped roadmap priorities across both content and product teams.

Retained through an 85% company reduction as the only non-Product/Engineering person kept at Thoughtful AI, on the basis of my ability to make complex AI systems legible across technical and non-technical stakeholders simultaneously.
jobDescriptionText:
Product Manager (Builder)
Company: Perplexity
Location: San Francisco / Palo Alto
Department: Product Management

PERPLEXITY IS LOOKING FOR PRODUCT MANAGERS TO JOIN A HIGHLY LEVERAGED, SMALL TEAM FOR BUILDING NEW PRODUCTS THAT INNOVATE TO ACCELERATE HUMAN PRODUCTIVITY.

In 2026, we launched Computer, the defining product for the new era of agentic AI. We've scaled beyond the millions of people using Perplexity every day for research, shopping, investing and curiosity into a new paradigm of using AI to transform knowledge into action.

Product managers at Perplexity work closely with design and engineering, focusing on the core Computer and search experience. The ideal candidate should have strong communication and writing skills, the ability to envision innovative technology, and willingness to reimagine work to be more efficient and creative. Ideal candidates have a passion in a particular industry that they are domain experts in: in the application please highlight which industry you are most interested in innovating.

WHAT YOU'LL DO
- Anticipate opportunities for innovation and value in enterprise industries.
- Envision new experiences and deliver a long-term vision.
- Understanding of levers for product-led growth and retention.
- Work closely with engineering and design to align product expectations and capabilities.
- Work with research to evaluate and steer nondeterministic models into the high value outcomes for users.
- Work with data and user research to understand quantitative and qualitative data.
- Have conviction to make difficult product decisions in the face of uncertainty.

QUALIFICATIONS
- Strong experience with product management and leadership.
- Strong experience working with data and metrics.
- Experience with productivity and knowledge work products.
- Experience with prototyping and data visualization.
- Experience with building data driven flywheels for iterative improvement.
- Thrives in a small, agile team: has initiative and desire for ownership.
- 4+ years of product management experience.

strengths.title: Used Perplexity to pitch Perplexity: the healthcare agent data layer thesis
strengths.description: For the application exercise, I didn't teach a hobby topic. I used Perplexity itself to argue that Perplexity should build the MCP-native healthcare agent data layer - an architecture that makes clinical data accessible to AI agents across payer and provider workflows without requiring custom integrations for every data source. The thesis was backed by investor and partner memos I had been developing for two years. This is the industry I know deeply, the product gap I can articulate from the ground up, and the exact kind of domain-expert conviction the JD is asking for when it says 'highlight which industry you are most interested in innovating.'

strengths.title: Production agentic AI systems with real eval infrastructure
strengths.description: I built a hybrid-RAG eligibility intelligence system at Thoughtful that went from zero to 100% accuracy on a labeled holdout dataset - embedding training data, building vector similarity retrieval, writing regression tests, and designing for convergent determinism as human-verified outputs accumulated. This wasn't a demo or prototype: it ran live on healthcare billing workflows with direct financial consequences for every error. That end-to-end AI product cycle - from architecture to eval to production - is exactly what Perplexity describes in steering nondeterministic models toward high-value user outcomes.

strengths.title: Principled framework for building on nondeterministic AI
strengths.description: I developed a design principle I called determinism-as-a-goal: use AI where genuine inference adds value, and write deterministic code everywhere else. This tradeoff framework - knowing when not to use a model - emerged from building real agentic systems where unpredictable outputs had financial consequences, and it became the architectural spine of every AI product decision I made at Thoughtful. Perplexity is doing the hardest version of this problem at scale, and I'd bring that same tradeoff discipline to every feature decision on the Computer and search roadmap.

strengths.title: Data-driven product feedback loops built from scratch
strengths.description: At Smarter Technologies I built a pre/post tool-use hook system that logged every agent skill invocation and surfaced user ratings on 15-20% of uses - not a third-party analytics integration, but a purpose-built feedback flywheel wired directly into the product. A cluster of thumbs-down responses on a specific skill told me exactly what to fix and why, shortening iteration cycles meaningfully. At Action Network I did the same thing at the metric layer - designing the Value Clicks model that connected editorial actions to affiliate LTV and became the north star that realigned both content and product roadmap priorities.

strengths.title: Deep analytics background with genuine product impact
strengths.description: My analytics work at FanDuel, Google, and Action Network wasn't reporting - it was cohort modeling, LTV frameworks, north star metric design, and cross-functional alignment around data that moved product decisions. At FanDuel I built the models that shaped acquisition and retention strategy for a consumer product at scale. At Action Network I connected in-product behavioral signals to downstream revenue, which is the same analytical motion I'd want to run at Perplexity: find the number that actually predicts the outcome you care about, then build the instrumentation to track it.

weaknesses.title: Limited formal PM title history against a 4+ year requirement
weaknesses.description: My formal Product Manager tenure was brief - roughly three months as TPM before moving into a delivery and sales engineering leadership track - which creates a real structural gap against the JD's 4+ year PM experience requirement.
weaknesses.mitigation: The substance of what Perplexity needs a PM to do - define what to build, sequence it against user signal, make tradeoffs under uncertainty, measure impact, and iterate - is exactly what I was executing as Lead TPM at Thoughtful. I drove 63% of net new ARR through those judgment calls, owned the product direction for a live agentic AI platform, and made scope decisions that affected both engineering velocity and customer outcomes. The title was different; the motion was the same. I'd also point to my Healthcare Agent Data Layer research project as two years of independent product vision work in the agentic AI space, done outside any formal role.

weaknesses.title: Domain background is healthcare and sports media, not knowledge work productivity
weaknesses.description: Almost all of my professional context is healthcare RCM automation and enterprise B2B sales engineering, with earlier work in consumer sports analytics - none of which maps directly to Perplexity's core ICP of knowledge workers and prosumer AI users.
weaknesses.mitigation: I'm an extremely heavy daily user of the exact category Perplexity is building for - I've built my own personal operating system in Notion, I use agentic AI as my primary work interface, and the agent infrastructure I built at Smarter Technologies was fundamentally a knowledge work productivity problem at its core. The healthcare framing was the customer context; the underlying design challenge - helping people offload cognitive work to AI agents reliably - is identical to what Perplexity is solving. I understand this user because I am this user, and I've been building tools for myself in this space for two years independently.

weaknesses.title: No direct consumer-scale PLG funnel or retention optimization experience
weaknesses.description: I haven't owned PLG loops or retention metrics for a horizontal consumer or prosumer SaaS product - my growth and retention work has been in enterprise B2B and media affiliate contexts, not self-serve consumer funnels.
weaknesses.mitigation: At Action Network I built the model that mapped in-product behavioral signals to predicted affiliate LTV, identified the highest-converting funnel path through the content experience, and watched it directly reshape roadmap priorities. That's the same analytical motion - connecting activation behavior to downstream retention and revenue - just applied to a different audience. At FanDuel I worked on acquisition and retention modeling for a consumer product at meaningful scale. I'd need to apply that framework to a horizontal prosumer product, which I'm confident I can do quickly given the underlying analytical mechanics are ones I've executed before.

referralBlurb:
For his application exercise, Dan didn't teach a hobby topic. He used Perplexity to argue that Perplexity should build the MCP-native healthcare agent data layer - an architecture connecting clinical data to AI agents across payer and provider workflows - backed by investor and partner memos he'd been developing for two years. That kind of conviction, applied to a real product gap in a domain he genuinely knows, is what the JD is asking for when it says candidates should highlight which industry they want to innovate in. He had the answer before the question was asked.

Beyond the exercise: Dan has actually built production agentic AI systems from the ground up - not managed vendors, not scoped prototypes, but written the embedding pipelines, vector retrieval logic, eval frameworks, and regression tests himself, then shipped them into live workflows with real financial consequences. At Thoughtful AI he drove 63% of net new ARR as Lead TPM by owning product direction for a live healthcare AI platform and moving a hybrid-RAG system from prototype to 100% accuracy on a labeled holdout dataset. He was retained through an 85% company reduction as the only non-Product/Engineering person kept - a clear signal about where leadership placed his product judgment.

His analytics work is genuine product depth: LTV and cohort modeling at FanDuel, north star metric design at Action Network (his Value Clicks model realigned both content and product roadmap priorities), and a pre/post tool-use hook feedback system at Smarter Technologies that logged every agent skill invocation and surfaced user ratings on 15-20% of uses. He builds data flywheels; he doesn't just consume them.

His formal PM title history is shorter than the JD targets and his domain background is healthcare rather than horizontal productivity software - both real gaps worth probing. But the PM motion Perplexity is hiring for - define what to build, sequence against user signal, make hard calls under uncertainty, measure impact - is what Dan was executing in practice. For a team that values builders who operate at both the product and technical layer, he's worth a conversation.

projects.name: Healthcare Agent Data Layer
projects.relevance: Two years of independent research into the MCP-native healthcare agent data layer - the product thesis I submitted as my application exercise, arguing that Perplexity should build the integration layer that makes clinical data accessible to AI agents across payer and provider workflows. This is the industry I know, the gap I can articulate precisely, and the product conviction the JD is asking for.

projects.name: Dental Eligibility Intelligence
projects.relevance: Production hybrid-RAG system that reached 100% accuracy on a labeled holdout dataset in a live healthcare billing workflow - direct evidence of steering nondeterministic models toward high-value, reliable outcomes, which is the core technical PM challenge at Perplexity.

projects.name: AI Meeting Intelligence
projects.relevance: Multi-agent pipeline that converts meeting recordings into structured deal intelligence with an automated feedback loop - a working data-driven product flywheel built from scratch, matching the JD qualification directly.
